hero.label: Made for Pier
hero.headline: I want to be the BDR who helps Pier own credit infrastructure.
hero.subheadline: Pier is doing for lending what Stripe did for payments, and that comparison is not hype, it is a real infrastructure gap being filled at exactly the right moment. I have spent the last year doing outbound BDR work in B2B SaaS and I know how to build pipeline from scratch. Working directly with Jessica to shape Pier's GTM motion from the ground up is the exact opportunity I have been looking for.

whyCompany.title: Credit-as-a-Service is a real infrastructure gap, not a trend
whyCompany.body: Most companies trying to embed lending are stuck navigating compliance, licensing, and API integrations on their own, which can take years. Pier collapses that into weeks by handling the hardest parts, compliance and infrastructure, so builders can focus on their customers. That is a genuinely strong value proposition to take to market.

whyCompany.title: First BDR hire at a YC W23 company approaching 7-figure ARR
whyCompany.body: Pier is not a pre-revenue experiment. It has traction, a raised round, and is now ready to scale outbound in a deliberate way. Being the first BDR at this stage means the work I do will directly shape the playbook, not just execute one someone else built.

whyCompany.title: My BDR experience maps directly to what this role requires
whyCompany.body: At Copygen.ai I ran cold outreach sequences in HubSpot and LinkedIn and helped the team acquire 15 to 20 paying clients from zero. Most recently at NextAfter I worked inside a B2B consultancy that sold to nonprofits, which sharpened how I communicate ROI to buyers who are skeptical by default. That combination of outbound execution and value-based messaging is exactly what building Pier's top-of-funnel requires.

quote: Jessica built Pier because she lived the problem herself, and that founder origin story tells me the product is solving something real. I want to be the person who gets that story in front of every company that is still trying to build credit infrastructure the hard way.
